questions.question: What is the primary operation performed on a Linked List when a new node is added?
options: Insert
Delete
Display
both Insert and Delete
questions.explanation: The correct option is 'Insert' as it is the primary operation performed on a Linked List when a new node is added.

questions.question: Which operation is performed on a Linked List to remove a node from the list?
options: Insert
Delete
Display
both Insert and Delete
questions.explanation: The correct option is 'Delete' as it is the operation performed on a Linked List to remove a node from the list.

questions.question: Why is the Display operation on a Linked List less efficient than Insert or Delete operations?
options: Because Linked Lists are not suitable for large datasets.
Because Linked Lists are not suitable for real-time data.
Because Linked Lists are less optimized for Display operation.
Because Linked Lists are too complex to be used for simple Display operations.
questions.explanation: The correct option is 'Because Linked Lists are less optimized for Display operation.' as the Display operation on a Linked List is less efficient because it requires traversing the entire list to display the data.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of using Linked Lists over Arrays for inserting or deleting elements in the middle of the list?
options: Arrays are more efficient for large datasets.
Arrays require more memory than Linked Lists.
Linked Lists can insert or delete elements in the middle of the list without shifting the entire array.
Arrays are more suitable for real-time data.
questions.explanation: The correct option is 'Linked Lists can insert or delete elements in the middle of the list without shifting the entire array.' as this is the main advantage of using Linked Lists over Arrays for inserting or deleting elements in the middle of the list.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'head' in a Linked List?
options: It represents the last element in the list.
It represents the first element in the list.
It is used to keep track of the current position of the cursor.
It is used to link the last node to the first node.
questions.explanation: The correct option is 'It represents the first element in the list.' as the 'head' in a Linked List represents the first element in the list and is used to access the first node in the list.
